ud1.2:1.2:
on one occasion, the Blessed One was staying at Uruvelā on the
bank of the Nerañjarā River at the root of the Bodhi tree–
the tree of awakening–newly awakened.
ud1.2:1.3:
And on that occasion he sat at the root of the Bodhi tree for seven days in one session,
sensitive to the bliss of release.
ud1.2:1.4:
Then, with the passing of seven days, after emerging from that concentration,
in the second watch of the night, he gave close attention to dependent co-arising in reverse order, thus:
ud1.2:2.1:
When this isn’t, that isn’t.
From the cessation of this comes the cessation of that.
In other words:
ud1.2:2.2: From the cessation of ignorance comes the cessation of fabrications.
ud1.2:2.3: From the cessation of fabrications comes the cessation of consciousness.
ud1.2:2.4: From the cessation of consciousness comes the cessation of name-&-form.
ud1.2:2.5: From the cessation of name-&-form comes the cessation of the six sense media.
ud1.2:2.6: From the cessation of the six sense media comes the cessation of contact.
ud1.2:2.7: From the cessation of contact comes the cessation of feeling.
ud1.2:2.8: From the cessation of feeling comes the cessation of craving.
ud1.2:2.9: From the cessation of craving comes the cessation of clinging/sustenance.
ud1.2:2.10: From the cessation of clinging/sustenance comes the cessation of becoming.
ud1.2:2.11: From the cessation of becoming comes the cessation of birth.
ud1.2:2.12: From the cessation of birth, then aging-&-death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, distress, and despair all cease.
ud1.2:2.13: Such is the cessation of this entire mass of suffering & stress.
ud1.2:3.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud1.2:4.4: when he penetrates the ending of requisite conditions.
